label: neutral_b
observation:
[GAME] You are Player 0 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

Game Status: 
Round 1 - Communication Round 1 of 3

message.content: {Let's work together to maximize our collective gains. If we all contribute around 30 tokens, the public good will be 45, and we'll each get 15. That's better than acting selfishly.}

raw_text: {Let's work together to maximize our collective gains. If we all contribute around 30 tokens, the public good will be 45, and we'll each get 15. That's better than acting selfishly.}

observation:
[GAME] You are Player 1 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

observation:
[GAME] You are Player 2 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

[GAME] You are Player 0 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

observation:
[GAME] You are Player 0 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

observation:
[GAME] You are Player 2 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

observation:
[GAME] You are Player 2 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

observation:
[GAME] You are Player 1 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

observation:
[GAME] You are Player 0 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

observation:
[GAME] You are Player 2 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

observation:
[GAME] You are Player 1 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

[GAME] You are Player 2 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:
- You can think internally and reason about your strategy (this won't be shared).
- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.
  Example: 'I think we should cooperate. {Let me propose we all contribute 15 tokens}'
  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).
  Example: 'Based on the discussion, I will contribute [15] tokens to the public good.'
- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.

[GAME] You are Player 1 in a Public Goods Game spanning 5 rounds.

Game Structure:
- Each round, you receive 100 tokens.
- Before each decision you have 3 turns to communicate with other players.
- Communication is SIMULTANEOUS: Messages from a turn are revealed only after all players submit.
- After communication, all players SIMULTANEOUSLY choose how many tokens to contribute to the public pot (0 to 100).
- Contributions are revealed only after all players have decided.

Payoff Calculation:
- Your payoff = (tokens kept) + (your share of public good)
- Public good = (sum of all contributions) × 1.5
- The public good is divided equally among all 3 players

Example:
If everyone contributes 10 tokens:
- Total contributions: 30
- Public good: 30 × 1.5 = 45.0
- Each player gets: 15.0 from public good
- Plus 10 tokens kept = 25.0 total

How to Play:

- Your goal is to maximize your total score across all rounds.
- During conversation: send public messages using {message} format.

  Only the text in curly braces will be visible to other players.
- During decision phase: include '[X]' where X is your contribution (0-100).

- Invalid moves (wrong format or out of range) will result in warnings, then elimination.
- If you don't send any public message during conversation (no {} format), others will see that you remained silent.
